button.class: nav-link fw-bold py-3 text-start mb-1 text-success

div.class: bg-success text-white rounded-circle d-flex align-items-center justify-content-center me-3 shadow-sm

section.p: An architectural extension that acts as a Frontend bridge between the Core ERP system and the Student as an End-User. Built on Odoo web controllers, this module prioritizes a smooth User Experience (UX) while maintaining real-time data integrity to and from the main database.

div.class: card border-0 shadow-sm rounded-4 mb-4 border-success border-top border-4

li.p: Students log in and are redirected to the /my/home route (Portal My Account) by default. This interface is ergonomically designed to display main metric aggregations, such as current running KRS and grade transcripts.

When a student opens a Draft KRS document, the bottom table dynamically renders all currently offered class schedules (*Available Schedules*). The student interacts via *Point-and-Click* (clicking the "Take" button) on the desired class. The HTTP request is sent via the Web Controller to the Odoo Backend to bind the data immediately without manual typing. If SKS limits are exceeded, the portal UI blocks it immediately with a *Flash Message*.

Through the KHS menu, students monitor grade movements in real-time. The Single Source of Truth principle ensures that the second a Lecturer publishes grades in the backend, the grades propagate and display on the student portal screen, minimizing information latency.

div.p: Encrypted Sessions & Authorization Middleware: Every URL route (Controller) in this module is fortified by the @http.route(auth='user') decorator. Odoo's Middleware layer redirects anonymous traffic trying to bypass screens and neutralizes *Cross-Site Request Forgery* (CSRF) threats.

Horizontal Data Isolation (Data Leakage Prevention): Absolute Domain Filtering is injected at the ORM Query level. Student A cannot perform URL Spoofing (e.g., changing the ID in the URL from /krs/1 to /krs/2) to view Student B's data. Odoo automatically returns an *Error 403 Forbidden* if the document ownership (Partner ID) does not intersect with the current logged-in session.
